cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about the World Bank study from 1974 mentioned in the text. The user is asking what the study found regarding economic growth in backward (developing) countries. Wait, the wording here is a bit tricky. The study said that the growth had been of little benefit to a third of their population. The original sentence in the text: "more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." So, the growth had little benefit to a third of the population. That means that only a third got the benefit, and the rest (two thirds) got little or none. So the study is saying that growth didn't benefit most people, only a third. So the correct answer would be that economic growth brought little benefit to their people.
